name: Obsidian Local REST API
what_it_does: A community plugin that spins up a local HTTPS REST server inside Obsidian, enabling external programs and agents to read, write, search, and manage vault content including notes, attachments, and metadata without leaving the local machine.
use_cases: Agents writing processed or summarized content directly into a local Obsidian vault
Reading vault content to build retrieval-augmented context for agents
Automating daily note creation and structured journaling
Ingesting external data (emails, web clips, research) into the vault
Querying vault search from agent pipelines
not_for: Remote or cloud-deployed agents (requires local network access to the running Obsidian app)
Production services that need SLA guarantees
best_when: An agent runs on the same machine (or local network) as Obsidian and needs to read/write the user's personal knowledge base.
avoid_when: Your agent runs in the cloud or a remote environment — the API is only accessible where Obsidian is open and running.

auth.notes: API key generated by the plugin, sent as Authorization: Bearer header. Self-signed TLS certificate for localhost HTTPS. No scopes — full vault access. Key is set once in plugin settings. Simple and practical for local agent use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Plugin is free and open source; Obsidian itself is free for personal use
pricing.paid_tiers: Obsidian Sync optional at $10/mo; Obsidian commercial license $50 one-time
pricing.notes: Zero cost for personal agent use. The plugin is MIT licensed. No API call limits, no metering — runs entirely locally.

performance.rate_limits: None enforced; constrained by local Obsidian app performance

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec is available and reasonably accurate. Error responses are basic HTTP status codes with short messages. Not always clear whether a 404 means file not found or path syntax error.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: File write operations are overwrite-by-default, which is effectively idempotent for full content replacement. Append operations are not idempotent. No explicit idempotency keys.
known_agent_gotchas: Obsidian must be open and running — the API is unavailable if the app is closed or sleeping
No uptime guarantee; local machine sleep/restart kills the server
Self-signed TLS certificate requires agents to disable cert verification or trust the cert explicitly
No webhooks — agents cannot be notified of vault changes
Path handling is vault-relative; absolute paths will fail
Large vaults with many files may have slow search response times
Plugin updates can introduce breaking changes with no migration path

score_rationale: For local agents, this plugin is excellent — zero cost, fast local latency, decent documentation, and an available MCP server. The fundamental constraint is the hard requirement for Obsidian to be open and running on the local machine, making it unsuitable for cloud agents. Within that constraint it is one of the best options for personal knowledge base automation. The community MCP server further lowers the integration barrier.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local API key for Obsidian REST plugin. HTTP on localhost only — do not expose externally. API key in config file — restrict file permissions. Community plugin, not official.
